channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey's Kurds wary of path to peace after PKK declares ceasefire
item.description: DIYARBAKIR, Turkey (AP) — A momentous ceasefire declaration on Saturday stirred a mix of emotions in southeast Turkey and northern Iraq, where people bore the brunt of the 40-year conflict between Kurdish militants and the Turkish state.
item.dc:creator: MUCAHIT CEYLAN and ADNAN AHMAD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day Saturday while others opened as usual as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan, the first since the fall of Assad family rule in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, The Hill

item.title: GOP governors rush to Trump's defense after ‘jarring’ spat with Zelensky
item.description: Red state governors were quick to throw their support behind President Trump following a tense back-and-forth with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office Friday afternoon, citing Trump's dedication to protecting Americans.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Namibian founding president Sam Nujoma is laid to rest and praised as the last African liberator
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia’s founding president, Sam Nujoma, who steered the country to freedom from apartheid South Africa, was laid to rest in the country’s Heroes Acre cemetery on Saturday following a state funeral attended by African leaders and foreign dignitaries.
item.dc:creator: SONJA SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas reports no progress in talks with Israel on ceasefire's second phase
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The latest round of talks on the second phase of the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has made “no progress,” and it's unclear whether they will resume on Saturday, a senior Hamas official said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kurdish militants declare a ceasefire in a 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey declared a ceasefire on Saturday in what could mark a significant boost to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government, two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of mourners attend funeral of Taliban-linked cleric killed in Pakistan suicide blast
item.description: AKORA KHATTAK, Pakistan (AP) — Thousands of mourners attended a funeral on Saturday for a Taliban-linked cleric slain in a suicide bomb attack on a seminary in Pakistan’s northwest.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia revels in Oval Office spectacle after Zelenskyy spars with Trump, Vance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's heated exchange with U.S. President Donald Trump has Russian leaders reveling in the spectacle, with one saying Kyiv got a "slap down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky seeks to thaw relations after Trump spat: Peace 'will be difficult' without US
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky took to social media Saturday to clarify comments he made during the contentious back-and-forth with President Trump at the White House a day earlier, saying lasting peace in Eastern Europe would be "difficult" without U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelensky-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: Palestinian businessman lobbies DC with long-shot Gaza vision
item.description: A Palestinian businessman is making a long-shot bid to lead a new governing body of the Gaza Strip, putting up an estimated $300,000 for lobbying services to influence Washington and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Pope has coffee, rests after setback in recovery
item.description: Pope Francis has had coffee and read the papers after an alarming setback in his two-week recovery from double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelenskyy-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: Ukrainians have rallied around President Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a defender of their country’s interests in the hours after he left the White House after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NY Republican: Putin is ‘the only one that won’ in Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) has called for President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to quickly resolve their disagreements after the explosive White House meeting, which he believes was detrimental to Ukraine's interests.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Trump-Zelensky clash highlights Congress at a crossroads over foreign policy
item.description: The explosive clash between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the White House on Friday has sparked a firestorm on Capitol Hill, pitting “America First” Republicans against Ukraine backers from both parties in the ferocious battle over the future of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Rose Girone, oldest living Holocaust survivor, dies at 113
item.description: Rose Girone, the oldest living survivor of the Holocaust and advocate for sharing the stories of survivors, died on Monday in New York at age 113.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: OULAD SLAMA, Morocco (AP) — Sheep come running when Larbi El Ghazouani pours alfalfa and straw into their troughs twice a day.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Zelenskyy to meet for key deal as NATO allies, Russia wait, watch
item.description: The world waits to see what could come from a key deal between Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and President Trump that could open Kyiv's minerals to the U.S. in exchange for security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says it performed cruise missile tests, days after it vowed to respond to US threats
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Friday it had test-fired strategic cruise missiles to demonstrate its nuclear counter-attack capability, days after it vowed to respond to what it called escalating U.S.-led hostilities since the start of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis' condition continues to improve as he receives oxygen therapy: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is improving as he undergoes high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said in a statement. The 88-year-old pontiff has been hospitalized for nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
